title: Outcomes of laparoscopic stapled anastomosis in colorectal surgery: a systematic review
authors: Hartmann J; Okafor C; Lindqvist P
findings_summary: Systematic review of 42 studies (n=8,114). Stapled anastomosis associated with low leak rates (2.9%) comparable to hand-sewn techniques; no device-specific failure pattern identified.

title: Mechanical failure modes of endoscopic linear staplers: a bench study
findings_summary: Bench analysis of 240 firing cycles across three stapler platforms. Misfire incidence of 0.8% under maximal articulation; recommends pre-fire articulation check in IFU.

title: Staple line bleeding after sleeve gastrectomy: incidence and management
authors: Moreau L; Singh R; Adeyemi T
findings_summary: Multicentre cohort (n=1,902). Staple line bleeding in 1.1% of cases; all managed intraoperatively. No association with any single stapler brand.

title: Comparative safety of powered versus manual laparoscopic staplers
findings_summary: Registry analysis of 5,400 procedures. Manual staplers showed marginally higher rate of incomplete firing strokes (0.4% vs 0.2%); clinical outcomes equivalent.

title: Human factors in laparoscopic stapler use errors: an observational study
journal: Journal of Patient Safety in Surgery
findings_summary: Observation of 312 procedures. 6 use errors observed, all related to cartridge selection and articulation; training intervention reduced errors by 60%.

title: Long-term tissue response to titanium staples: histological follow-up
findings_summary: Histology at 24 months post-implantation showed stable encapsulation and no chronic inflammatory response attributable to titanium staples.

title: Trends in surgical stapler adverse event reporting to MAUDE 2018-2023
findings_summary: MAUDE reporting for surgical staplers declined 18% since 2019 labelling changes; misfire remains the most-reported device problem category industry-wide.

title: Cost-effectiveness of single-use laparoscopic instruments
findings_summary: Economic model comparing single-use and reusable laparoscopic instruments; no safety endpoints assessed.
